examples.title: Supervised Learning: Linear Regression
examples.description: Train a simple linear regression model using gradient descent.
examples.code:
# y = w*x + b (simple linear regression)
xs = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
ys = [2, 4, 5, 4, 5]

w, b = 0.0, 0.0
lr = 0.01
epochs = 1000

for _ in range(epochs):
 dw = 0.0
 db = 0.0
 n = len(xs)
 for x, y in zip(xs, ys):
 y_hat = w * x + b
 dw += (y_hat - y) * x
 db += (y_hat - y)
 w -= lr * (2 / n) * dw
 b -= lr * (2 / n) * db

print(f"Learned parameters: w={w:.2f}, b={b:.2f}")
print(f"Prediction for x=6: {w*6 + b:.2f}")
examples.explanation: Supervised learning uses labeled (x, y) pairs. Gradient descent minimizes mean squared error to learn w and b.

examples.title: Unsupervised Learning: K-Means (2D)
examples.description: Cluster points into k groups without labels.
examples.explanation: K-means clusters unlabeled data by iteratively assigning points to the nearest centroid and recomputing centroids.

examples.title: Reinforcement Learning: Epsilon-Greedy Bandit
examples.description: Learn to choose the best arm using reward feedback.
examples.explanation: The agent explores with probability epsilon and exploits the best-known arm otherwise, updating value estimates from rewards.
